prompt: I would like a detailed analysis of the Saint Seiya franchise (anime/manga). The analysis should be structured around the different classes of armor (Cloths, Scales, Surplices, God Robes, etc.), such as Bronze Saints, Silver Saints, Gold Saints, Marina Generals, Specters, God Warriors, etc. For each significant character within these categories, provide details on their power level, signature techniques, key appearances/story arcs, and final outcome/fate within the series.
article:
### Scope, canon assumptions, and evidence limits (important)
- **Primary scope assumed (per your request):** Masami Kurumada’s *Saint Seiya* manga, Toei’s **classic TV anime**, and the **Hades OVAs** (Sanctuary / Inferno / Elysion).  
- **Also included because you explicitly asked:** the **Asgard arc** (anime-original) and its **God Robes / God Warriors**, plus the **Poseidon arc** (Scales / Marina Generals) and **Hades arc** (Surplices / Specters / Judges / divine twins). The analysis is **structured around armor classes** and the in-universe factions who wear them.
- **Critical limitation:** the research history you provided contains **excellent system-level info** (how Cloths/Scales/Surplices/God Robes work) and **good coverage for many Poseidon/Hades/Asgard characters**, but it is **missing** reliable, fully sourced technique/fate pages for *many* classic characters—especially:
  - many **Silver Saints** (Marin, Shaina, Misty, etc.) and most **Gold Saints’** signature moves and detailed fates,
  - **Hades himself** and several **key Hades-arc endgame events**,
  - some **Sea Generals** (Krishna is partial; several are absent).
- Where I *do* include character techniques/outcomes, they are **only** what is supported by the captured sources. Where sources are missing, I mark it explicitly rather than guessing.

---

## 1) Armor taxonomy and “power” mechanics (how the franchise organizes strength)

### 1.1 Cosmo: the universal “engine” behind techniques
- **Cosmo** (小宇宙) is described as the fundamental inner energy present in all living beings, originating from the Big Bang; “burning” it recreates a micro-scale Big Bang effect and enables superhuman combat feats. It is also influenced by emotion and willpower surges. [[4]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Cosmo)
- **Seventh Sense**: framed as awakening the “true potential” of Cosmo; associated with **light-speed** combat and star-level destructive language in the referenced material, and is strongly associated with **Gold Saints**, though some lower-ranked Saints can reach it too. The core protagonists are stated to awaken Seventh Sense during the **12 Zodiac Temples** battle. [[4]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Cosmo)
- **Eighth Sense (Arayashiki)**: described as a “Cosmo of the soul,” necessary to function freely in the Underworld; extremely difficult to awaken at death. [[4]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Cosmo) (Note: in the captured record this is explained mainly via a secondary wiki summary, so treat it as a *compiled* explanation rather than an official databook quote.) [[4]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Cosmo)

### 1.2 Cloths (Athena’s Saints) — Bronze / Silver / Gold + special evolutions
- The series’ canonical count is presented as **88 Cloths**, divided into **48 Bronze, 24 Silver, 12 Gold**. [[1]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Cloths)
- **Gold Cloths** are described as the highest class, worn by Saints who have mastered **Seventh Sense**; they are “alive” (reacting through the wearer’s Cosmo), associated with **light-speed** combat, can “freeze at absolute zero,” and are extremely durable—stated to require a god in a true body to fully destroy one. [[2]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Gold_Cloths)
- Gold Cloths’ zodiac logic: Gold constellations lie along the Sun’s path and the Cloths have “absorbed the light of the Sun since the days of Myth.” [[2]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Gold_Cloths)
- **Silver Saints’ institutional role** (from an “Encyclopedia” translation): Silver Saints execute Sanctuary’s global missions; Gold Saints are the “last resort.” Silver Cloths are usually defense-focused (few “extra features”) because Silver Saints rely on their own trained combat skill, unlike Bronze Cloths which often have special functions aiding their support role. [[71]](https://philipho.tripod.com/SSaint.html)

### 1.3 God Cloths (Divine Cloths) — Bronze Saints’ Cloths transformed by Athena’s blood
- God Cloths are described as a unique, myth-age class that manifests by **Athena’s blood** spilled on the original Cloths, with power surpassing Gold Saints and enabling combat against minor gods like **Thanatos** and **Hypnos**. [[5]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/God_Cloths)
- An official Bandai/Tamashii product page explicitly corroborates the concept “God Cloth manifests through the goddess’s blood” (女神の血により発現する ‘神聖衣’). [[6]](https://tamashiiweb.com/item/1337)
- God Cloths are presented as near the apex of “mortal-made” Cloths, second only to Olympian “Kamui.” [[5]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/God_Cloths)

### 1.4 Scales (Poseidon’s forces)
- Scales are armors of Poseidon’s Marinas; Cloths are stated to have been born to **counteract** the devastating powers of Poseidon’s Scales. [[17]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Scales)
- Scales contain **Orichalcum** (“mountain copper”). [[17]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Scales)
- “Marine General Scales” are repeatedly described as the **Gold Saint equivalent** in the captured sources. [[22]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Sea_Horse_Scale) [[24]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Scylla_Scale) [[26]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Kraken_Scale) [[28]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Lyumnades_Scale)

### 1.5 Surplices (Hades’ Specters)
- Surplices are the Underworld armors worn by Hades’ **108 Specters**; described as equivalent (category-wise) to Cloths, Scales, God Robes, etc. [[33]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Surplices)
- Surplices are made from Underworld minerals and represent monsters from myth and literature. [[33]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Surplices)
- A key functional distinction: Surplices allow Specters to travel to/from the Underworld without burning Cosmo to Eighth Sense levels. [[33]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Surplices) The Underworld itself is described as uninhabitable for the living without Eighth Sense or special means; Specters are exempt due to Surplices and Hades’ permission. [[35]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Underworld)

### 1.6 God Robes (Asgard arc, anime-original)
- **God Robes** are the armors of Odin’s **God Warriors** in the **Asgard arc** (episodes **74–99**, anime-original). [[19]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_Saint_Seiya_episodes) [[54]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/God_Robes)
- They are ancient, awakened by Hilda (under the Nibelungen Ring’s influence) through Polaris power. [[54]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/God_Robes)
- Each Robe has an **Odin Sapphire**; collecting all seven enables summoning **Odin’s Robe** and the sword **Balmung**, used to remove/destroy the Nibelungen Ring’s control. [[54]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/God_Robes) [[55]](https://www.themoviedb.org/tv/42444-saint-seiya/episode_group/5c85f9cdc3a3684e98ddd519/group/5c85fa53c3a3687f84d24385) [[56]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Odin_Robe)
- God Warriors are described as exceptionally strong, with baseline comparable to **Gold Saints**. [[58]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/God_Warriors)

### 1.7 Kamui (Olympian god armors) and Athena’s Cloth
- “Kamui” are described as the original sacred armors of the 12 Olympian gods; the captured record claims only **Athena’s Cloth** is shown in battle on several occasions and is central in wars vs Hades. [[36]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Kamui)
- Athena’s Cloth can enter a statue-like dormant state and can be restored by Athena’s blood. [[36]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Kamui)

---

## 2) How “power level” is assessed here (method)
Because Saint Seiya’s “power” is narrative-driven and Cosmo-dependent, the most defensible approach from the captured sources is:
1. **Rank + armor class** (Bronze/Silver/Gold; General Scale ≈ Gold Saint; Judges ≈ Gold Saints, etc.). [[2]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Gold_Cloths) [[22]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Sea_Horse_Scale) [[41]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Wyvern_Rhadamanthys)
2. **Sense milestones** (Seventh Sense; Eighth Sense; “Divine/God Cloth” awakenings). [[4]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Cosmo) [[5]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/God_Cloths) [[35]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Underworld)
3. **Matchups and situational rules** (Underworld barriers reducing Saints’ Cosmo; special environments like Hyperdimension; weaponized Cloths). [[41]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Wyvern_Rhadamanthys) [[45]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Basilisk_Sylphid) [[7]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Libra_weapons) [[9]](https://www.mfi.mn/serial/saint-seiya-the-hades-chapter/inferno-part-2/desperation-the-wailing-wall/detail)
4. **Named technique portrayal** (what the story says a technique does, and what it destroys/overcomes). [[41]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Wyvern_Rhadamanthys) [[43]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Garuda_Aiacos) [[45]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Basilisk_Sylphid) [[46]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Alraune_Queen)

---

# 3) Athena’s Saints — Cloth-based factions

## 3.1 Bronze Saints (main protagonists; Cloth → God Cloth progression)
The captured sources explicitly identify the five protagonists and their Seventh Sense awakening at the Twelve Temples. [[4]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Cosmo)

### Pegasus Seiya — Pegasus Bronze Cloth → Pegasus God Cloth
- **Armor class:** Bronze Cloth (Pegasus), later **God Cloth** (Pegasus). [[12]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_Saint_Seiya_chapters_(series)) [[5]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/God_Cloths)
- **Power portrayal:**
  - Awakens **Seventh Sense** during the Twelve Temples. [[4]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Cosmo)
  - God Cloth durability is illustrated by the claim Thanatos’ Terrible Providence (which destroyed multiple Gold Cloths) could not scratch Pegasus God Cloth. [[5]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/God_Cloths)
- **Signature technique (explicitly named in captured material):**
  - *Pegasus Meteor* (“Meteor of Pegasus”) is named in a third-party episode summary describing Seiya counterattacking Thanatos. [[13]](https://www.mfi.mn/serial/saint-seiya-the-hades-chapter/elysium/legendary-god-cloths/detail) (This is a non-official recap; treat as a secondary reference.)
- **Key arcs/appearances (captured):**
  - Wins Pegasus Cloth and enters Galaxian Wars (manga summary). [[12]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_Saint_Seiya_chapters_(series))
  - Poseidon arc and Hades Elysion events are referenced indirectly through Cloth transformations and God Cloth context. [[5]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/God_Cloths) [[13]](https://www.mfi.mn/serial/saint-seiya-the-hades-chapter/elysium/legendary-god-cloths/detail)
- **Fate/outcome:** not explicitly stated in the captured sources (only battle events and armor damage examples are described). [[5]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/God_Cloths) [[13]](https://www.mfi.mn/serial/saint-seiya-the-hades-chapter/elysium/legendary-god-cloths/detail)

### Dragon Shiryu — Dragon Bronze Cloth → (God Cloth exists in concept)
- **Armor class:** Bronze Saint (Dragon). [[12]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_Saint_Seiya_chapters_(series))
- **Power portrayal:** Awakens Seventh Sense in Twelve Temples battle (group statement). [[4]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Cosmo)
- **Key arc note (captured manga summary):** goes to Jamir for Cloth repair; loses much blood to repair Cloths, becomes critically weak; later fights Black Dragon. [[12]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_Saint_Seiya_chapters_(series))
- **Fate/outcome:** not captured.

### Cygnus Hyoga — Cygnus Bronze Cloth → Cygnus God Cloth (product-confirmed existence)
- **Armor class:** Bronze Saint (Cygnus). [[12]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_Saint_Seiya_chapters_(series))
- **Power portrayal:** Awakens Seventh Sense in Twelve Temples battle (group statement). [[4]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Cosmo)
- **God Cloth:** the existence of “Cygnus Hyoga God Cloth” is corroborated by an official Tamashii Nations product page (merch context), which also reinforces “God Cloth manifests by goddess’s blood.” [[6]](https://tamashiiweb.com/item/1337)
- **Fate/outcome:** not captured.

### Andromeda Shun — Andromeda Bronze Cloth (chains)
- **Armor class:** Bronze Saint (Andromeda). [[12]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_Saint_Seiya_chapters_(series))
- **Power portrayal:** Seventh Sense awakening in Twelve Temples battle (group statement). [[4]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Cosmo)
- **Key arc note:** identified in manga summaries as using Andromeda Chains; later forced to defeat Black Andromeda. [[12]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_Saint_Seiya_chapters_(series))
- **Fate/outcome:** not captured.

### Phoenix Ikki — Phoenix Bronze Cloth (self-restoration concept is mentioned generally)
- **Armor class:** Bronze Saint (Phoenix). [[12]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_Saint_Seiya_chapters_(series))
- **Power portrayal:** Seventh Sense awakening in Twelve Temples battle (group statement). [[4]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Cosmo)
- **Key arc note:** steals the Gold Cloth during early arcs; commands Black Saints; later reconciles with others. [[12]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_Saint_Seiya_chapters_(series))
- **Fate/outcome:** not captured.

**System note:** a guide excerpt explicitly uses Phoenix Cloth’s self-restoration as an example of Bronze Cloth “extra features” beyond protection. [[71]](https://philipho.tripod.com/SSaint.html)

---

## 3.2 Silver Saints (Silver Cloths; “experts of war”)
### What Silver Saints *are* institutionally (official guide excerpt)
- Silver Saints are Sanctuary’s primary mission executors: globally deployed, reliable, and often leading missions where Bronze Saints act as support. [[71]](https://philipho.tripod.com/SSaint.html)
- Silver Cloths tend to be **purely defensive** compared with many Bronze Cloths which have special functions; Silver Saints rely on developed martial skill rather than Cloth gimmicks. [[71]](https://philipho.tripod.com/SSaint.html)

### Notable Silver Saint explicitly supported by an official channel
#### Eagle Marin — Silver Saint; Seiya’s mentor figure
- **Official characterization:** Toei Animation’s official X account calls Marin a “Silver Saint and Seiya’s mentor.” [[76]](https://x.com/ToeiAnimation/status/1902078617787465868)
- **Hades OVA appearance evidence:** ANN cast list for Hades Chapter Inferno includes a credited role “Aquila Marin.” [[70]](https://www.animenewsnetwork.com/encyclopedia/anime.php)
- **Techniques / fate:** not reliably provided in official sources within the captured record. (A fan profile page lists multiple attacks for Marin, but it is not an official databook in the captured evidence.) [[72]](http://www.geocities.ws/shun_andromeda_saint/characters/silver.html)

> **Important gap:** The captured record does not contain official technique lists or definitive end-of-story outcomes for Silver Saints like Marin/Shaina/Misty/etc. The only official-style material captured for Silver Saints is role/structure and mission logic. [[71]](https://philipho.tripod.com/SSaint.html) [[76]](https://x.com/ToeiAnimation/status/1902078617787465868)

---

## 3.3 Gold Saints (Gold Cloths; Seventh Sense standard)
### Gold Cloth baseline traits (applies to all Gold Saints)
- Given to exceptionally powerful Saints who mastered Seventh Sense; Gold Saints are typically light-speed fighters. [[2]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Gold_Cloths)
- Gold Cloths are “alive,” extremely durable, associated with absolute zero resistance, and can endure huge Cosmo outputs. [[2]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Gold_Cloths)

### Major Sanctuary Gold Saints (the canonical Twelve)
A non-official character guide list in the captured record enumerates the Twelve: Aries Mu, Taurus Aldebaran, Gemini Saga, Cancer Deathmask, Leo Aiolia, Virgo Shaka, Libra Dohko, Scorpio Milo, Sagittarius Aiolos, Capricorn Shura, Aquarius Camus, Pisces Aphrodite. [[3]](http://saintseiya.itgo.com/goldsaints.html) (This list matches the commonly accepted Twelve, but note: this particular source is a fan site.)

Because **individual Gold Saints’ signature techniques and their Sanctuary-arc fates** are not comprehensively captured via official databook pages in this evidence set, the entries below focus on **what is explicitly supported**.

---

### Shared “team techniques” / systems relevant to Gold Saints

#### Athena Exclamation (forbidden triad technique)
- Described as an ultimate forbidden technique: three Gold Saints combine Cosmo to unleash Big Bang-equivalent power; Athena banned it as dishonorable (three vs one). [[11]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Athena_Exclamation)  
  *Note:* this is from a wiki page in the captured record rather than a primary scan; treat it as a compiled explanation. [[11]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Athena_Exclamation)
- “Known users” listed in the captured material:  
  - Gemini Saga + Capricorn Shura + Aquarius Camus  
  - Aries Mu + Leo Aiolia + Scorpio Milo [[11]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Athena_Exclamation)

#### Libra weapons (unique Gold Cloth armory)
- Libra Gold Cloth uniquely contains multiple weapon pairs; captured lists include **two swords, two shields, two tridents, two tonfas** (snippet truncated beyond that). [[7]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Libra_weapons)
- Dohko’s Libra Cloth is stated to contain **6 pairs of weapons** to be used by Gold Saints. [[8]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Libra_Dohko)
- A Hades OVA episode summary states Dohko distributed Libra weapons among the Gold Saints to destroy the **Wailing Wall**. [[9]](https://www.mfi.mn/serial/saint-seiya-the-hades-chapter/inferno-part-2/desperation-the-wailing-wall/detail) (Third-party recap source.)

#### Sagittarius Bow and Arrow
- Sagittarius Gold Cloth includes a **Golden Bow and Arrow** made of the same material as the Cloth. [[10]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Sagittarius_Cloth)

---

### Individual Gold Saints with notable captured facts

#### Aquarius Camus — Gold Saint; also appears in Hades arc as a Surplice-wearer
- **Techniques (explicitly listed in captured infobox):** Diamond Dust, Koltso, Freezing Coffin, Aurora Execution, Athena Exclamation. [[47]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Aquarius_Camus)
- **Official promotional support for signature move name:** a Toei “Soul of Gold” official page describes “Aurora Execution” as his greatest technique approaching absolute zero. [[77]](https://www.saintseiya-gold.com/en/character.html) (This is spinoff promotional text, but it is an official Toei statement about the move.)
- **Hades arc role:** part of the trio (Saga/Shura/Camus) that confronts Shaka and uses Athena Exclamation. [[48]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Capricorn_Shura)
- **Outcome/fate evidence:** Camus’s Seiyapedia infobox lists multiple “places of death,” including **Judecca (Underworld)**, implying an ultimate death there in Hades context, but the detailed narrative is not captured. [[47]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Aquarius_Camus)

#### Capricorn Shura — Gold Saint; Hades arc “revived” participant
- **Hades arc actions (captured):** with Saga and Camus, fights Shaka; Shaka forces them toward Athena Exclamation; they unleash it, killing Shaka; later their Athena Exclamation is countered by another Athena Exclamation. [[48]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Capricorn_Shura)
- **Outcome/fate:** not captured.

#### Gemini Saga — Gold Saint; Hades arc revived participant
- **Hades arc involvement (captured indirectly):** part of the Athena Exclamation trio against Shaka. [[48]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Capricorn_Shura)
- **Power portrayal claims (captured):** Seiyapedia claims Saga is said to be the most powerful Gold Saint of his generation (referencing *Next Dimension*) and also makes a “semi-canon” Soul of Gold claim. [[49]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Gemini_Saga) (These are not primary manga quotes in the captured record.)

#### Virgo Shaka — Gold Saint; Underworld insight role
- **Eighth Sense association:** Shaka is described as “closest man to the gods” due to Eighth Sense mastery in the compiled Cosmo explanation. [[4]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Cosmo)
- **Wailing Wall sequence:** a Hades OVA episode summary describes Shaka deducing Hades’ true body is in Elysium, attempting to create sunlight via Cosmo expansion, and the rosary’s beads reflecting Specter losses. [[9]](https://www.mfi.mn/serial/saint-seiya-the-hades-chapter/inferno-part-2/desperation-the-wailing-wall/detail) (Third-party recap source.)
- **Outcome/fate:** not captured definitively in the captured evidence.

#### Libra Dohko — Gold Saint; weapons custodian
- **Libra Cloth weapons:** 6 weapon pairs; unique full weapon armory. [[8]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Libra_Dohko) [[7]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Libra_weapons)
- **Wailing Wall:** distributes weapons for Wailing Wall destruction (recap). [[9]](https://www.mfi.mn/serial/saint-seiya-the-hades-chapter/inferno-part-2/desperation-the-wailing-wall/detail)
- **Outcome/fate:** not captured.

#### Aries Mu / Leo Aiolia / Scorpio Milo — Athena Exclamation trio (Hades arc)
- **Athena Exclamation usage list:** Mu/Aiolia/Milo named as users. [[11]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Athena_Exclamation)
- **Wailing Wall recap:** Athena’s Cosmos revives Aiolia, Mu, Milo at the Wailing Wall (recap). [[9]](https://www.mfi.mn/serial/saint-seiya-the-hades-chapter/inferno-part-2/desperation-the-wailing-wall/detail)
- **Outcome/fate:** not captured.

#### Taurus Aldebaran — benchmarked by Seiya’s Cosmo
- A compiled Cosmo page caption claims Seiya’s Seventh Sense burning surpassed Aldebaran’s Cosmos (caption-style statement). [[4]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Cosmo)
- Further details not captured.

#### Sagittarius Aiolos (Aiolos/Aioros)
- **Sagittarius Cloth weapon:** Golden Bow and Arrow. [[10]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Sagittarius_Cloth)
- A recap claims Aiolos’ spirit sent his Cloth to help Saints in Elysion. [[13]](https://www.mfi.mn/serial/saint-seiya-the-hades-chapter/elysium/legendary-god-cloths/detail) (Third-party recap.)
- Fate not captured.

#### Cancer Deathmask & Pisces Aphrodite — Hades arc humiliation by Rhadamanthys
- Rhadamanthys page describes revived Deathmask and Aphrodite failing their mission, dominating lesser Specters, then being confronted by Rhadamanthys: their strongest techniques had no effect; Rhadamanthys reflects Deathmask’s Underworld waves and sends both to the Underworld. [[41]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Wyvern_Rhadamanthys)
- Aphrodite page snippet claims it is hinted Aphrodite could use Eighth Sense by the end of Hades arc (snippet is truncated and incomplete). [[50]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Pisces_Aphrodite)

---

# 4) Poseidon faction — Scales (Marinas / Sea Generals)

## 4.1 Arc structure and Scale hierarchy
- **Poseidon arc** (classic anime) is episodes **100–114**. [[19]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_Saint_Seiya_episodes)
- Poseidon’s Undersea Temple is supported by **seven giant pillars** (Mammoth Pillars), each guarded by a **Marina General**; to save Athena and stop the flood, Saints must defeat each General and destroy each pillar, then confront the Main Breadwinner where Athena is held. [[20]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Mermaid_Thetis)
- Multiple Scale pages explicitly call a **Marine General Scale** the **Gold Saint equivalent**. [[22]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Sea_Horse_Scale) [[24]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Scylla_Scale) [[26]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Kraken_Scale) [[28]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Lyumnades_Scale)
- Scales are linked to **Orichalcum** composition in the broader system description. [[17]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Scales)

---

## 4.2 Poseidon (Julian Solo) — Poseidon Scale (personal)
- **Role:** Julian Solo is the host of Poseidon reincarnated in the modern era. [[21]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Julian_Solo)
- **Armor:** Poseidon Scale, worn by Julian as Poseidon’s avatar. [[18]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Poseidon_Scale)
- **Durability portrayal (captured claim):** the Poseidon Scale is portrayed as substantially more resistant than typical General Scales; it took a Sagittarius Gold Cloth arrow imbued with the Cosmo of four Bronze Saints to pierce its headpiece. [[18]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Poseidon_Scale)  
  *(This is a fandom page claim in the captured record; its internal citations are not visible here.)* [[18]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Poseidon_Scale)
- **Outcome/fate:** not captured in primary form here; a secondary Poseidon-arc summary indicates Athena reseals Poseidon and Julian collapses unconscious after the battle (secondary site summary). [[91]](http://www.saintseiyapedia.com/wiki/Pos%C3%A9idon)

---

## 4.3 Mermaid Thetis — Mermaid Scale (lower-rank Marina)
- **Rank/armor:** Mermaid Scale; explicitly “lower rank than the Generals.” [[20]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Mermaid_Thetis)
- **Debut (captured):** Chapter 46 / Episode 100. [[20]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Mermaid_Thetis)
- **Signature technique (captured):** *Deathtrap Coral*. [[20]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Mermaid_Thetis)
- **Key appearances/role:** catalyst and messenger—guides Julian into Poseidon’s destiny; informs Saints of Athena’s confinement; tries to block Shaina/Kiki’s delivery of Libra Cloth; defeated by Shaina but not killed. [[20]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Mermaid_Thetis)
- **Fate/outcome:**  
  - Manga-ending description: Julian later encounters a coral-colored fish implied to be Thetis. [[20]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Mermaid_Thetis)  
  - Anime-ending description: Thetis saves Julian from collapsing temple and returns to the sea. [[20]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Mermaid_Thetis)

---

## 4.4 Sea (Marina) Generals — the “Gold Saint equivalent” Scale-bearers

### Sea Horse Baian — Sea Horse Scale (North Pacific Pillar)
- **Armor/rank:** Sea Horse Scale; Marine General; guardian of North Pacific Pillar; “Gold Saint equivalent.” [[22]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Sea_Horse_Scale)
- **Combat portrayal:** fights Seiya; initially blocks attacks and even throws Seiya through the ocean; Seiya discovers a secret and defeats him; last seen with broken Scale falling. [[22]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Sea_Horse_Scale)
- **Techniques:** the captured page says he uses three major techniques, but their names are not included in the captured excerpt. [[22]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Sea_Horse_Scale)
- **Fate/outcome:** defeat implied; death not explicitly stated. [[22]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Sea_Horse_Scale)

### Scylla Io — Scylla Scale (South Pacific Pillar)
- **Armor/rank:** Scylla Scale; Marine General; guardian of South Pacific Pillar; “Gold Saint equivalent.” [[24]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Scylla_Scale)
- **Debut:** manga volume 16; anime episode 102. [[23]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Scylla_Io)
- **Signature techniques (explicit list):**
  - Eagle Clutch, Wolf’s Fang, Queen Bee’s Stinger, Serpent Strangler, Vampire Inhale, Grizzly Slap, Big Tornado. [[24]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Scylla_Scale)
- **Key battle/arc:** prolonged fight vs Andromeda Shun; Shun’s chains adapt to each weapon-form; Io’s Big Tornado pushes Shun to extreme Cosmo and Shun’s Cloth radiates gold (attributed to Gold blood used to resurrect it). [[23]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Scylla_Io)
- **Fate/outcome:** Io sacrifices himself by taking a direct hit from a Libra weapon strike, but fails to stop the pillar’s destruction; dies warning Shun about mercy. [[23]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Scylla_Io)

### Kraken Isaac — Kraken Scale (Arctic Ocean Pillar)
- **Armor/rank:** Kraken Scale; Marine General; guardian of Arctic Ocean pillar; “Gold Saint equivalent.” [[26]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Kraken_Scale)
- **Signature technique:** *Aurora Borealis*—described as almost on par with Camus’ Aurora Execution but somewhat weaker; stronger than most of Hyoga’s techniques. [[25]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Kraken_Isaak) [[26]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Kraken_Scale)
- **Key arc:** deeply tied to Hyoga as a former friend; saved by Poseidon’s will after being swept away; returns as ruthless General; battles Hyoga; targets Kiki; Hyoga’s Seventh Sense returns and Hyoga ultimately uses Aurora Execution to destroy Isaac’s Scales. [[25]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Kraken_Isaak)
- **Fate/outcome:** dies after defeat, congratulating Hyoga and warning him about the next enemy (Sea Dragon). [[25]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Kraken_Isaak)

### Lyumnades Caça — Lyumnades Scale (Antarctic Ocean Pillar)
- **Armor/rank:** Lyumnades Scale; Marine General; guardian of Antarctic Ocean pillar; “Gold Saint equivalent.” [[28]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Lyumnades_Scale)
- **Debut:** Chapter 55 / Episode 107. [[27]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Lyumnades_Ca%C3%A7a)
- **Signature techniques:** *Salamander Shock* and *Thunder Veil*. [[27]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Lyumnades_Ca%C3%A7a)
- **Combat profile:** psychological/illusion warfare (“Hunter of Hearts”); creates illusions of loved ones; deceives Seiya and Hyoga; nearly breaks Shun by transforming into Ikki; Phoenix Ikki arrives and defeats him. [[27]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Lyumnades_Ca%C3%A7a)
- **Fate/outcome:** Ikki punches through his heart and defeats him with *Houyoku Tenshou*; dies at the pillar. [[27]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Lyumnades_Ca%C3%A7a)

### Siren Sorrento — Siren Scale (South Atlantic Pillar)
- **Armor/rank:** Marina General; guardian of South Atlantic Ocean pillar. [[29]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Siren_Sorrento)
- **Signature techniques:**
  - *Dead End Symphony* (signature flute song; attacks even if unheard by resonating in the brain; can shatter chain/Cloth by “music alone” in the description),  
  - *Dead End Climax*,  
  - plus anime-only *Circular Barrier* and *Sound Waves*. [[29]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Siren_Sorrento)
- **Key appearances:**
  - Manga: fights Taurus Aldebaran at hospital; stalemate; later escorts Saori to Julian; begins respecting Athena. [[29]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Siren_Sorrento)
  - Anime: appears earlier in Asgard as Poseidon messenger; fights Siegfried with Dead End Symphony; Siegfried dies. [[29]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Siren_Sorrento)
  - Poseidon arc: battles Shun; Shun ultimately uses Nebula Storm; Sorrento survives. [[29]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Siren_Sorrento)
- **Fate/outcome:** survives; later helps Ikki destroy North Atlantic pillar after realizing Kanon manipulated the war; after Poseidon is sealed, travels with Julian to help disaster victims and appears again in Hades arc. [[29]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Siren_Sorrento)

### Sea Dragon Kanon — Sea Dragon Scale (North Atlantic Pillar)
- **Armor/rank:** Sea Dragon Scale; Kanon masquerades as Sea Dragon General; explicitly stated he wore this Scale in the original series. [[31]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Sea_Dragon_Scale) [[30]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Sea_Dragon_Kanon)
- **Power/role portrayal:** architect-level antagonist:
  - directly responsible for inciting Saga’s fall and for Poseidon’s resurrection/partial awakening, keeping Poseidon from fully awakening so Kanon could control the Marinas. [[30]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Sea_Dragon_Kanon)
  - survives Cape Sounion watery prison repeatedly due to a “powerful yet gentle Cosmo” lifting him (later identified in-story by Ikki as Athena’s Cosmo). [[30]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Sea_Dragon_Kanon)
- **Techniques:** not captured in the provided sources.
- **Fate/outcome:** the captured Kanon narrative is truncated right as it describes him taking Poseidon’s trident through his chest to protect Athena, so final outcome is **not** captured. [[30]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Sea_Dragon_Kanon)

### Chrysaor Krishna — Chrysaor Scale (Indian Ocean Pillar) *(partial)*
- **Armor/rank:** Chrysaor Scale; Marine General; guardian of Indian Ocean pillar. [[32]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Chrysaor_Scale)
- **Technique captured:** *Maha Roshni* is identified as an offensive technique used against Shiryu (description truncated). [[32]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Chrysaor_Scale)
- **Further appearances/fate:** not captured in the provided excerpt set.

# 5) Hades faction — Surplices (Specters / Judges) + divine Underworld beings

## 5.1 Hades arc scope (manga and anime)
- The Hades arc is described as the final chapter of Kurumada’s manga, corresponding to **volumes 19–28**, with a first half focused on Gold Saints at the Twelve Temples and a second half in the Underworld led by the main Bronze team. [[92]](https://dic.pixiv.net/a/%E3%83%8F%E3%83%BC%E3%83%87%E3%82%B9%E7%B7%A8)
- Toei’s official portal provides release windows for the Hades OVAs: Sanctuary (2002–2003), Inferno 1 (2005–2006), Inferno 2 (2006–2007), Elysion (2008). [[93]](https://www.saintseiya-anime.com/en/history/)

---

## 5.2 Surplices and Underworld environment mechanics
- Surplices: Underworld mineral armor, monster-themed, enabling Underworld traversal without Eighth Sense-level Cosmo. [[33]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Surplices)
- Underworld: souls judged by the Three Judges; structure described as nine prisons and Dante-inspired geography; Elysion exists as a divine zone accessible only to those chosen by gods. [[35]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Underworld)

---

## 5.3 The Three Judges of Hell (Celestial Specters)

### Wyvern Rhadamanthys — Wyvern Surplice
- **Rank:** one of the **3 Judges** (with Minos and Aiacos); powerhouse comparable to Gold Saints. [[41]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Wyvern_Rhadamanthys)
- **Signature techniques (captured list):** Greatest Caution, Wyvern’s Roar, Greeding Roar. [[41]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Wyvern_Rhadamanthys)
- **Key feats/appearances (captured):**
  - Overpowers revived Gold Saints Deathmask and Aphrodite; reflects Deathmask’s Underworld waves and sends them to the Underworld. [[41]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Wyvern_Rhadamanthys)
  - Fights Aiolia/Mu/Milo 3v1 under Hades castle barrier conditions where Saints’ Cosmo is reduced to 10% while Rhadamanthys remains at 100%. [[41]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Wyvern_Rhadamanthys)
- **Fate/outcome:** not captured (page truncates before later events). [[41]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Wyvern_Rhadamanthys)

### Griffon Minos — Griffon Surplice
- **Rank:** Judge; power source Celestial Noble Star. [[42]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Griffon_Minos)
- **Signature technique:** *Cosmic Marionation* (listed as his primary attack). [[42]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Griffon_Minos)
- **Key appearance note:** uses Cosmic Marionation on Kanon after Aiacos hits him with Galactica Illusion (described on Aiacos page). [[43]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Garuda_Aiacos)
- **Fate/outcome:** not captured. [[42]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Griffon_Minos)

### Garuda Aiacos — Garuda Surplice
- **Rank:** Judge; power source Celestial Valiance Star. [[43]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Garuda_Aiacos)
- **Signature techniques (captured):** Garuda Flap, Galactica Illusion, Galactica Death Bring, Surendrajit. [[43]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Garuda_Aiacos)
- **Key battle:** fights Phoenix Ikki; Ikki adapts (“same technique won’t work twice” logic is explicitly used in this narrative); Ikki ultimately defeats him with *Hou Yoku Ten Sho*. [[43]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Garuda_Aiacos)
- **Fate/outcome:** explicitly described as decisively destroyed/obliterated by Ikki. [[43]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Garuda_Aiacos)

---

## 5.4 Notable Specters (non-Judges) with strong captured coverage

### Papillon Myu — Papillon Surplice (Terrestrial Specter)
- **Techniques (captured):** Ugly Eruption, Silky Thread, Fairy Thronging. [[44]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Papillon_Myu)
- **Key arc:** fights Aries Mu; metamorphosis/larval phases; uses fairies for tracking; Mu uses Crystal Net and ultimately *Starlight Extinction* to send Myu to his death. [[44]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Papillon_Myu)
- **Fate/outcome:** dies by Mu’s Starlight Extinction. [[44]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Papillon_Myu)  
  *(Source quality note: this page is flagged “Citations needed” in the captured record; some details are marked citation-needed.)* [[44]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Papillon_Myu)

### Basilisk Sylphid — Basilisk Surplice
- **Techniques (captured):** Annihilation Flap (wind), Poisonous Wind (noted in another continuity context on the same page). [[45]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Basilisk_Sylphid)
- **Key arc:** fights Shiryu at Wailing Wall sequence; survives initial exchanges, but later pursues into the Hyperdimension where only gods or those with divine blood can survive; Sylphid is crushed by the currents. [[45]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Basilisk_Sylphid)
- **Fate/outcome:** dies in Hyperdimension currents. [[45]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Basilisk_Sylphid)

### Alraune Queen — Alraune Surplice
- **Technique:** *Blood Flower Scissors* (guillotine-like decapitation imagery; whip-like stems). [[46]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Alraune_Queen)
- **Key arc:** fights Shiryu; Shiryu counters with Excalibur; later Queen is killed by Shiryu’s maximum Cosmo *Rozan Hyaku Ryu Ha*. [[46]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Alraune_Queen)
- **Fate/outcome:** dies to Rozan Hyaku Ryu Ha. [[46]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Alraune_Queen)

---

## 5.5 Divine beings in Elysion: Hypnos and Thanatos (divine Surplices)

### Hypnos — god of Sleep (divine Surplice)
- **Role:** twin of Thanatos; subordinate of Hades; strategic and calm. [[37]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Hypnos)
- **Signature technique:** *Eternal Drowsiness* (deep slumber; reversion possible via deity blood; can be neutralized by extreme Cosmo in the captured narrative). [[37]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Hypnos)
- **Licensed corroboration of move name:** Tamashii Nations explicitly calls his special move “エターナルドラウジネス” (Eternal Drowsiness). [[97]](https://x.com/t_features/status/1420927751066820612)
- **Key arc actions:** appears in Elysion during Seiya’s arrival; stops Thanatos; imprisons Athena in Sacred Amphora via slumber; uses Eternal Drowsiness on Shun; is ultimately defeated when Saints’ Cloths resurrect into “Divine” states and their Cosmo shatters his Surplice and obliterates him. [[37]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Hypnos)
- **Fate/outcome:** body obliterated after Surplice shatters. [[37]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Hypnos)

### Thanatos — god of Death (divine Surplice)
- **Role:** cruel, merciless; destroys multiple Gold Cloths in a single attack; wields “Big Will” (divine Cosmo). [[38]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Thanatos)
- **Signature technique:** *Terrible Providence* (blast that cripples body and mind, annihilates senses, induces insanity, dismembers; used on Ikki in the described portrayal). [[38]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Thanatos)
- **Key arc:** battles Seiya in Elysion; destroys multiple Cloths; later Seiya’s Cloth resurrects into Divine state (via Athena blood/extreme Cosmo) and Seiya defeats Thanatos, destroying his Surplice and killing him. [[37]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Hypnos) [[38]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Thanatos)
- **Fate/outcome:** “met his end” at Seiya’s hands (in the captured narrative). [[37]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Hypnos)

> **Armor-class insight:** The captured material treats Hypnos/Thanatos Surplices as divine-blood-soaked but not true “Kamui.” [[33]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Surplices) It also treats “Divine Cloth / God Cloth” awakening (via Athena blood) as the Saints’ necessary escalation to defeat them. [[37]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Hypnos) [[5]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/God_Cloths)

---

## 5.6 Athena’s Cloth (Kamui) in the Hades endgame
- Athena’s Cloth is described as the key Kamui shown in battle; it enables full divine Cosmo and can sustain massive punishment from Hades. [[36]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Kamui)
- It can be restored by Athena’s blood and may exist dormant as the Sanctuary Athena statue. [[36]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Kamui)
- In the captured Elysion narrative, Seiya arrives to Elysion to deliver Athena’s Cloth; Thanatos takes it; Seiya later retrieves it after Divine Cloth awakening. [[37]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Hypnos)

---

## 5.7 “Revived Gold Saints” as Surplice-wearers (Hades arc infiltration force)
The captured record includes partial, but important, evidence about some revived Gold Saints.

### Aquarius Camus — Aquarius Surplice
- **Surplice listed:** Aquarius Surplice. [[47]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Aquarius_Camus)
- **Techniques listed:** Diamond Dust, Koltso, Freezing Coffin, Aurora Execution, Athena Exclamation. [[47]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Aquarius_Camus)
- **Key Hades arc involvement:** Athena Exclamation vs Shaka alongside Saga and Shura. [[48]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Capricorn_Shura)
- **Fate/outcome:** implied eventual death at Judecca per infobox; narrative not captured. [[47]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Aquarius_Camus)

### Capricorn Shura / Gemini Saga (revived trio member)
- **Key involvement:** uses Athena Exclamation with Saga and Camus against Shaka; later their Athena Exclamation is countered by another Athena Exclamation. [[48]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Capricorn_Shura)
- **Fate/outcome:** not captured in the provided evidence.

### Cancer Deathmask & Pisces Aphrodite
- **Key event captured:** both are judged/overpowered by Rhadamanthys after failing mission; sent to the Underworld. [[41]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Wyvern_Rhadamanthys)
- Aphrodite Eighth Sense hint is present only as a truncated snippet. [[50]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Pisces_Aphrodite)

# 6) Asgard faction (anime-original) — God Robes / God Warriors / Odin Robe

## 6.1 Arc and armor-system rules
- Asgard arc is anime-original and runs **episodes 74–99**. [[19]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_Saint_Seiya_episodes)
- God Robes are awakened by Hilda under the Nibelungen Ring; each has an Odin Sapphire; collecting all seven enables summoning Odin’s Robe and sword **Balmung** to remove the ring. [[54]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/God_Robes) [[55]](https://www.themoviedb.org/tv/42444-saint-seiya/episode_group/5c85f9cdc3a3684e98ddd519/group/5c85fa53c3a3687f84d24385) [[56]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Odin_Robe)
- God Warriors’ baseline is described as comparable to **Gold Saints**, and God Robes are portrayed as tougher than Silver Cloths but likely below Gold Cloths in some respects. [[58]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/God_Warriors) [[54]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/God_Robes)

---

## 6.2 Odin’s Robe (final key armor)
- **Odin’s Robe:** armor of Odin; Seiya temporarily wears it to destroy/remove the Nibelungen Ring and free Hilda. [[56]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Odin_Robe)
- **Balmung:** legendary sword contained/associated with Odin’s Robe, needed to remove the ring (per episode synopsis logic). [[55]](https://www.themoviedb.org/tv/42444-saint-seiya/episode_group/5c85f9cdc3a3684e98ddd519/group/5c85fa53c3a3687f84d24385) [[56]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Odin_Robe)

---

## 6.3 Major Asgard characters (God Warriors + Hilda)

### Polaris Hilda (High Priestess; antagonist under control)
- **Role:** Odin’s representative; manipulated by Poseidon via the Nibelungen Ring. [[53]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Asgard) [[54]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/God_Robes)
- **Key arc beat:** freed after Seiya obtains Odin’s Robe/Balmung and removes/destroys the ring’s control. [[59]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Polaris_Hilda) [[56]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Odin_Robe)
- **Fate/outcome:** liberated; further post-arc fate not captured.

---

## 6.4 The Seven God Warriors (God Robes)

### Dubhe Alpha Siegfried — Dubhe Alpha God Robe
- **Power portrayal:** last and strongest defender; defeats Seiya and then Ikki/Shun/Hyoga using an “invisible shield” defense and *Odin Sword*. [[61]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Dubhe_Alpha_Siegfried)
- **Techniques:**  
  - *Odin Sword* (laser-like attack from ground debris projection),  
  - *Dragon Bravest Blizzard* (two-headed dragon-shaped freezing wind; exposes chest momentarily). [[61]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Dubhe_Alpha_Siegfried)
- **Key arc:** confronts Seiya at Odin statue; later fights Sorrento; dies during that confrontation. [[61]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Dubhe_Alpha_Siegfried)
- **Fate/outcome:** dies (after Sorrento encounter). [[61]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Dubhe_Alpha_Siegfried)

### Merak Beta Hagen — Merak Beta God Robe
- **Technique captured:** *Universe Freezing*. [[55]](https://www.themoviedb.org/tv/42444-saint-seiya/episode_group/5c85f9cdc3a3684e98ddd519/group/5c85fa53c3a3687f84d24385)
- **Power portrayal:** trained in extreme volcanic region; masters ice and fire, withstands extreme heat; overwhelms Hyoga until defeat. [[55]](https://www.themoviedb.org/tv/42444-saint-seiya/episode_group/5c85f9cdc3a3684e98ddd519/group/5c85fa53c3a3687f84d24385)
- **Fate/outcome:** implied dead by episode 83 synopsis (Hyoga makes a grave for Hagen). [[55]](https://www.themoviedb.org/tv/42444-saint-seiya/episode_group/5c85f9cdc3a3684e98ddd519/group/5c85fa53c3a3687f84d24385)

### Phecda Gamma Thor — Phecda Gamma Jormungandr Robe
- **Techniques:** *Titanic Hercules*; *Mjolnir Hammer*. [[64]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Phecda_Gamma_Thor) (Mjolnir Hammer also appears in episode synopsis.) [[55]](https://www.themoviedb.org/tv/42444-saint-seiya/episode_group/5c85f9cdc3a3684e98ddd519/group/5c85fa53c3a3687f84d24385)
- **Power portrayal:** dominates early group fight; Seiya ultimately defeats him after Thor’s Cosmo surges. [[64]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Phecda_Gamma_Thor)
- **Fate/outcome:** dies after Seiya tears through his torso; acknowledges hope of saving Hilda. [[64]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Phecda_Gamma_Thor)

### Alioth Epsilon Fenrir — Alioth Epsilon God Robe
- **Techniques:** *Wolf Cruelty Claw*; *Northern Wolf Pack Punch*. [[65]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Alioth_Epsilon_Fenrir)
- **Key arc:** ideological fight vs Shiryu; Shiryu triggers avalanche burying both. [[65]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Alioth_Epsilon_Fenrir) [[55]](https://www.themoviedb.org/tv/42444-saint-seiya/episode_group/5c85f9cdc3a3684e98ddd519/group/5c85fa53c3a3687f84d24385)
- **Fate/outcome:** dies in avalanche; wolves find body. [[65]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Alioth_Epsilon_Fenrir) [[55]](https://www.themoviedb.org/tv/42444-saint-seiya/episode_group/5c85f9cdc3a3684e98ddd519/group/5c85fa53c3a3687f84d24385)

### Benetnasch Eta Mime — Benetnasch Eta God Robe
- **Techniques:** *Stringer Requiem*; “Lightspeed Attack” (as named/described on the character page). [[67]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Benetnasch_Eta_Mime)
- **Key arc:** Shun’s chains can’t treat him as enemy; Ikki reveals Mime’s suppressed malice; Ikki finishes him. [[66]](https://thetvdb.com/series/saint-seiya/episodes/96019) [[67]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Benetnasch_Eta_Mime) [[55]](https://www.themoviedb.org/tv/42444-saint-seiya/episode_group/5c85f9cdc3a3684e98ddd519/group/5c85fa53c3a3687f84d24385)
- **Fate/outcome:** killed by Ikki (per character-page narrative). [[67]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Benetnasch_Eta_Mime)

### Mizar Zeta Syd & Alcor Zeta Bud — Zeta God Robe + replica (Bud)
- **Armor system nuance:** Bud’s robe is a **replica** without an Odin Sapphire; explicitly stated not considered a true God Robe, yet Bud fights at God Warrior level, supporting that the Sapphire doesn’t itself grant combat power. [[54]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/God_Robes)
- **Bud’s technique:** *Shadow Viking Tiger Claw* (stronger variant of Syd’s). [[68]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Alcor_Zeta_Bud)
- **Key arc:** Bud secretly supports Syd; helps defeat Aldebaran by surprise; later fights and loses will to fight after Ikki reads his feelings. [[68]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Alcor_Zeta_Bud)
- **Fate/outcome:** implied he dies in snow beside Syd near birthplace (implication, not an explicit on-screen confirmation in the captured text). [[68]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Alcor_Zeta_Bud)
- **Syd:** detailed techniques/outcome not captured in the provided evidence set beyond roster/system references. [[53]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Asgard) [[54]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/God_Robes)

### Megrez Delta Alberich — Megrez Delta God Robe *(partial)*
- **Unique armor property (captured):** Delta Robe gauntlets store acid (the only explicit special property noted among God Robes in the captured system page). [[54]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/God_Robes)
- **Techniques / fate:** not captured in the provided evidence.

---

# 7) Cross-faction comparative insights (what the armor classes imply)

## 7.1 “Equivalence tiers” suggested directly by the captured sources
- **Gold Saints ↔ Seventh Sense mastery** is a core association: Gold Cloths are granted to Saints who mastered Seventh Sense, and Gold Saint speed is framed as light-speed. [[2]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Gold_Cloths)
- **Marine Generals ≈ Gold Saints** is repeatedly stated: Sea General Scales are explicitly called the “Gold Saint equivalent.” [[22]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Sea_Horse_Scale) [[24]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Scylla_Scale) [[26]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Kraken_Scale) [[28]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Lyumnades_Scale)
- **God Warriors ≈ Gold Saints** is stated at the group level for Asgard: baseline comparable to Gold Saints. [[58]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/God_Warriors)
- **Judges ≈ Gold Saints** is implied by Rhadamanthys: “comparable to Athena’s Gold Saints,” with the Judges operating as the Underworld’s top commanders. [[41]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Wyvern_Rhadamanthys)

## 7.2 Environment and rule-based power modifiers matter as much as raw rank
- Underworld/Hades castle barrier: Gold Saints’ Cosmo reduced to 10% while Rhadamanthys remains at 100% due to Hades’ territory effect—an explicit “situational scaling” example. [[41]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Wyvern_Rhadamanthys)
- Hyperdimension survival: only gods or those with divine blood can safely traverse; this “divine blood” rule kills Sylphid. [[45]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Basilisk_Sylphid)
- Cloth evolution via Athena’s blood is a decisive “power leap” enabling battles against divine beings (Hypnos/Thanatos). [[5]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/God_Cloths) [[37]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Hypnos)

## 7.3 Weaponized armor is rare but strategically decisive
- Libra weapons are a structural “break-glass” toolkit among Gold Saints; they are distributed specifically for something only gods can normally pass (the Wailing Wall). [[8]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Libra_Dohko) [[9]](https://www.mfi.mn/serial/saint-seiya-the-hades-chapter/inferno-part-2/desperation-the-wailing-wall/detail)
- Sagittarius Bow/Arrow is an explicit ranged god-tier piercing tool in multiple arcs’ iconography, referenced for Poseidon Scale penetration claims. [[10]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Sagittarius_Cloth) [[18]](https://saintseiya.fandom.com/wiki/Poseidon_Scale)

# 8) What’s still missing vs. your requested “for each significant character” level of completeness
To fully satisfy your requested format for *every major character* (especially **all Gold Saints**, the complete **Sea Generals**, full **Specter rosters**, and detailed **final fates**), the captured research record would need additional **primary/official character guide pages** or licensed encyclopedias that list:
- each Gold Saint’s **signature techniques**, key Sanctuary + Hades arc beats, and explicit fates,
- each Sea General’s full technique list (Krishna, Kanon, and several others are incomplete here),
- Hades’ own detailed final battle techniques and defeat sequence (not captured in the provided sources),
- a more authoritative Silver Saint dataset beyond general role description. [[71]](https://philipho.tripod.com/SSaint.html)

What *is* comprehensive in the provided evidence is the **armor-system taxonomy** and the **Poseidon/Asgard/Hades (Hypnos/Thanatos + several Judges/Specters)** character clusters, for which the sources include concrete technique names and outcomes.
